name: nc_no_evidence
description: Step 1.65 deterministic short-circuit (no-evidence gate) — tagged_evidence is None, so the orchestrator returns a deterministic Non-Compliant WITHOUT consulting the LLM. Verdict policy (2026-06-17, owner decision): a control with no implementation evidence is a finding the assessor runs down at end-of-assessment, not Unknown. This SUPERSEDES the prior abstain behavior (Step D, 2026-06-11), which was anchored to an eval ('88% of rule_no_evidence rows disagreed with gold') that turned out to be a tagger/retrieval artifact of one workbook — the engine failed to retrieve evidence that existed — not a durable truth. The gate routes through _finalize_no_evidence_decision: status=NON_COMPLIANT, source='rule_no_evidence', needs_review=False, confidence=1.0, review_reason prefixed 'no-evidence:'. The narrative carries accepted gap phrases ('No evidence found', 'POA&M') so the validator classifies it as a legitimate finding. The empty stub queue confirms the LLM is not called; any call would raise AssertionError from the stub.

ccis_row.guidance: The organization assigns account managers for information system accounts.
ccis_row.procedures: Examine evidence of designated account managers.
